title: A Multiagent-Driven Robotic AI Chemist Enabling Autonomous Chemical Research On Demand
recommended_pdf_url_or_status: Best current legal-PDF lead is the accepted author manuscript listed by University of Birmingham at https://research.birmingham.ac.uk/files/284954774/entry_3.pdf ; the landing page is accessible and labels it accepted author manuscript, but direct PDF fetch returned 403 here; final ACS PDF route also returned 403
concrete_scientific_objects: functional materials; photocatalytic organic reactions; robotic chemistry experiments
concrete_object_evidence: The accessible Birmingham repository record mirrors the ACS abstract and states that a hierarchical multiagent ChemAgents system runs seven tasks, with six ending in functional-material discovery/optimization and a seventh performing photocatalytic organic reactions; this keeps chemistry and materials object evidence both concrete
safety_or_access_note: No safety issue; ACS publisher landing/PDF and ACS figshare supplementary route were access-blocked in this environment, and the Birmingham direct PDF link also returned 403 even though the repository page clearly lists an accepted author manuscript
short_reason: A stronger first-hand lead now exists via the institutional-repository accepted-manuscript record, but no full manuscript PDF was actually retrievable here, so the landed 03;04 reading stays stable and source_limited should remain yes

title: Cellvoyager: Ai compbio agent generates new insights by autonomously analyzing biological data
recommended_pdf_url_or_status: No legal main-paper PDF obtained; strongest accessible file is the official Nature supplementary PDF at https://static-content.springer.com/esm/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41592-026-03029-6/MediaObjects/41592_2026_3029_MOESM1_ESM.pdf ; Nature PDF route falls back into preview/cookie flow and bioRxiv landing/PDF remained 403
concrete_scientific_objects: scRNA-seq datasets; CellBench benchmark studies; COVID-19 immune-response single-cell data; human endometrium cell-cell-communication data; aging brain single-cell data
concrete_object_evidence: Nature preview states CellVoyager autonomously generates and implements scRNA-seq analyses in Jupyter, is evaluated on 76 published scRNA-seq studies in CellBench, and reports three case studies in COVID-19, cell-cell communication, and aging; the Nature data/code-availability sections and official GitHub README reinforce these single-cell computational-biology objects
safety_or_access_note: No safety issue; Nature article preview and official supplementary PDF are accessible, but Nature main-paper PDF is not directly obtainable in this environment and bioRxiv landing/PDF both returned 403
short_reason: This round produced a stronger first-hand source via the official supplementary PDF, which reinforces a stable 06 single-cell/computational-biology anchor; 07 pressure remains only a future lead, not a landed module

title: Autonomous Nanocrystal Doping by Self-Driving Fluidic Micro-Processors
first_hand_sources_checked: DOI landing https://doi.org/10.1002/aisy.202200017 -> official Wiley article https://advanced.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/aisy.202200017 ; Crossref publisher metadata+abstract https://api.crossref.org/works/10.1002/aisy.202200017 ; official Wiley PDF link in Crossref https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/pdf/10.1002/aisy.202200017 ; official Wiley page shows full-text sections and supporting-information entry
recommended_pdf_url_or_status: Archive official Wiley VOR PDF: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/pdf/10.1002/aisy.202200017 ; Crossref records CC BY 4.0 license for VOR, so legal local archiving appears allowed
concrete_scientific_objects: lead halide perovskite nanocrystals; sequential halide exchange; metal-cation doping; target nanocrystal doping levels
concrete_object_evidence: Crossref abstract and official Wiley article listing show an autonomous fluidic micro-processor running sequential halide-exchange and cation-doping campaigns on LHP nanocrystals, building a surrogate model, and completing five closed-loop synthesis campaigns for target doping levels
safety_or_access_note: No safety issue. On 2026-07-03 automated terminal fetches to Wiley hit Cloudflare challenge, but publisher-side evidence is materially stronger than the current note state: official article/full-text presence, supporting-info listing, CC-BY license, and official PDF URL are all confirmed.
short_reason: Direct optimized object is nanocrystal materials composition/doping, not a general method; this now looks archiveable and no longer genuinely source-limited.
